scenario: Junction Logistics dispatches 7000 shipments per day on Camunda 8.8 and stores a pickup at @"2020-07-31T14:27:30@Europe/Berlin" on the process instance. A modeller needs the documented FEEL call from the conversion functions page that returns the same instant rendered in the local time of the America/Los_Angeles timezone for a downstream carrier service worker.
question: Which call on the conversion functions page returns @"2020-07-31T14:27:30@Europe/Berlin" rendered in America/Los_Angeles local time?

options.text: convert timezone(@"2020-07-31T14:27:30@Europe/Berlin", "America/Los_Angeles"); the docs document a convert timezone() function that adjusts the local time to match the target timezone on the conversion functions page

at timezone(@"2020-07-31T14:27:30@Europe/Berlin", "America/Los_Angeles"); the docs document an at timezone() function that adjusts the local time to match the target timezone on the conversion functions page

date and time(@"2020-07-31T14:27:30@Europe/Berlin", "America/Los_Angeles"); the docs document date and time(date: date and time, timezone: string): date and time, marked as a Camunda Extension, and show it returning date and time("2020-07-31T05:27:30@America/Los_Angeles")

time zone shift(@"2020-07-31T14:27:30@Europe/Berlin", "America/Los_Angeles"); the docs document a time zone shift() function that adjusts the local time to match the target timezone on the conversion functions page

a.text: Incorrect. The conversion functions page does not document a convert timezone() function; the documented function that returns a date and time at a target timezone is date and time(date, timezone).

b.text: Incorrect. The conversion functions page does not document an at timezone() function; the documented function that returns a date and time at a target timezone is date and time(date, timezone).

c.text: Correct. The conversion functions page documents date and time(date: date and time, timezone: string): date and time as a Camunda Extension. If the date argument has a different timezone than the target timezone, it adjusts the time to match the local time, and shows date and time(@"2020-07-31T14:27:30@Europe/Berlin", "America/Los_Angeles") returning date and time("2020-07-31T05:27:30@America/Los_Angeles").

d.text: Incorrect. The conversion functions page does not document a time zone shift() function; the documented function that returns a date and time at a target timezone is date and time(date, timezone).

explanation: date and time(date: date and time, timezone: string): date and time is documented on the conversion functions page as a Camunda Extension. If the date argument has a different timezone than the target timezone, it adjusts the time to match the local time of the target. The page shows the Europe/Berlin to America/Los_Angeles example returning date and time("2020-07-31T05:27:30@America/Los_Angeles").
